creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
All images, which are embedded in a web-page, which
	Firefox3 had to resize (because the originals are larger)
	appear either fully black or mostly corrupted.

	DrudgeReport.com, apparently, relies on the browser resizing
	the images a lot -- here is the snapshot of the window, so
	you can see, what I'm talking about:

		http://aldan.algebra.com/~mi/tmp/black-images.png

	Even if part of an image appears properly, scrolling down
	in the window promptly replaces each picture with a black
	rectangle...

	I wonder, if this is due to some local misconfiguration here,
	or if that's because I have amd64 and there is a bug in ff
	somewhere (or Gnome?)...

Fix: 

Go back to firefox-2.x
